mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 8.0%, consumer spending power collapses and recession probabilities spike, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE crosses above 8.0%, or the signal loses predictive power during high-inflation periods.

mechanism_narrative: Rising real sales of autos and light trucks combined with stable consumer price inflation indicates robust consumer demand without monetary erosion, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when RSAFS growth exceeds CPIAUCSL growth by more than 2 percentage points, or interaction term IC is negative.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production signals immediate capacity utilization improvements and earnings revisions, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week during expansion phases.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration yields negative or flat 5-day forward returns during late-cycle phases, or the signal predictive accuracy drops below 52% in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Increasing housing starts reflect rising household confidence and construction sector activity, which translates into positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: HOUST increases consistently precede negative 60-day SPX returns, or the correlation coefficient remains below 0.05 across multiple rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid monthly inflation spikes trigger expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive when CPIAUCSL exceeds the 0.4% threshold, or the signal shows no statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in total nonfarm payrolls and declining unemployment rates confirms broad-based labor market strength, supporting corporate revenue growth and positive S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal predicts negative returns when PAYEMS growth outpaces UNRATE decline by more than 1.5 standard deviations, or interaction IC is negative.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP contractions signal immediate recessionary conditions that force earnings downgrades, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter during downturn phases.
failure_condition: GDPC1 contractions are followed by positive 60-day SPX returns in more than 60% of historical instances, or the signal fails to separate from market noise in regime-filtered tests.
